# @pegasus-heavy/nestjs-platform-deno

> NestJS HTTP adapter for the Deno runtime using Deno.serve

A NestJS HTTP adapter that lets you run NestJS applications directly on Deno's native HTTP server (`Deno.serve`) without requiring Express, Fastify, or any other Node.js HTTP framework. It serves as a drop-in replacement for `@nestjs/platform-express` and `@nestjs/platform-fastify`, bringing full NestJS compatibility to the Deno ecosystem with zero external HTTP framework dependencies.

## Links

- [Documentation](https://pegasusheavy.github.io/nestjs-deno/)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/pegasusheavy/nestjs-deno)
- [JSR Package](https://jsr.io/@pegasus-heavy/nestjs-platform-deno)

## Key Features

- Native Deno HTTP server support via `Deno.serve`
- Full NestJS compatibility (controllers, DI, guards, interceptors, pipes, exception filters)
- Zero external HTTP framework dependencies
- Built-in CORS support
- Static file serving with configurable options
- Express middleware compatibility (helmet, compression, morgan, etc.)
- Fastify hooks and plugin compatibility (@fastify/cors, @fastify/helmet, etc.)
- Custom error and 404 handler support
- TypeScript first

## Installation

```bash
# Using Deno
deno add jsr:@pegasus-heavy/nestjs-platform-deno npm:@nestjs/common npm:@nestjs/core

# Using npm/pnpm (for Node.js + Deno interop)
pnpm add @pegasus-heavy/nestjs-platform-deno @nestjs/common @nestjs/core
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { DenoAdapter } from '@pegasus-heavy/nestjs-platform-deno';
import { AppModule } from './app.module.ts';

async function bootstrap() {
  const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule, new DenoAdapter());
  await app.listen(3000);
}

bootstrap();
```
